methods.description: Compilation of planktonic foraminifera species' stratigraphic ranges from published sources, calibration to a geologic timescale, and binning into 1 Ma intervals for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: compilation of 699 planktonic foraminifera species' stratigraphic ranges from published sources
steps.searchable_summary: Compile species stratigraphic ranges from published literature
inputs: Published sources of planktonic foraminifera stratigraphic ranges
outputs: Raw stratigraphic range data for 699 species

steps.description: calibrated to Gradstein et al., (2012) geologic timescale
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate stratigraphic data to a specific geologic timescale
Bin calibrated stratigraphic data into 1 million year intervals
outputs: Binned stratigraphic range data (1 Ma intervals)

methods.description: Calculation of standing diversity using the boundary-crossing (BC) methodology based on binned stratigraphic ranges, preferred over subsampling methods due to data availability limitations.

steps.description: These stratigraphic ranges were used to determine standing diversity... analyzed using both the boundary-crossing (BC; Bambach, 1999) ... methodologies
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate standing diversity using the boundary-crossing method
inputs: Binned stratigraphic range data (1 Ma intervals)
outputs: Standing diversity values per 1 Ma interval

steps.description: For each curve, data were computed at 1 Ma intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Compute diversity data for each 1 Ma interval
inputs: Binned stratigraphic range data (1 Ma intervals)
outputs: Standing diversity data series (1 Ma resolution)

methods.description: Calculation of extinction and origination rates using the per-capita rates technique based on first and last appearances of taxa within 1 Ma intervals, selected because the raw data is based on first and last appearances and a comprehensive occurrence database is not available.

steps.description: raw data for the computation of extinction and origination rates was based on first and last appearances of the taxa
steps.searchable_summary: Extract first and last appearance data from binned stratigraphic ranges
inputs: Binned stratigraphic range data (1 Ma intervals)

steps.description: the per-capita-rates technique for calculating extinction and origination rates (Foote, 2000) was selected
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate per-capita extinction and origination rates using first and last appearance data

steps.description: Per-capita rates were used as they address those sorts of biases by drawing a census of taxa at precise moments in time (i.e., the beginning and the end of the interval)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform taxon census at interval boundaries for per-capita rate calculation
inputs: Binned stratigraphic range data (1 Ma intervals)
outputs: Taxon counts at the beginning of each interval
Taxon counts at the end of each interval

methods.description: Application of first-generalized differences to the datasets (turnover rates and standing diversity) to avoid spurious correlations resulting from serial auto-correlation and 'noisy' secular trends before performing statistical correlation.

steps.description: the datasets were analyzed using first-generalized differences before the use of the statistical correlation to compare turnover rates (i.e., extinction versus origination)
steps.searchable_summary: Compute first-generalized differences for turnover rate datasets

steps.description: the datasets were analyzed using first-generalized differences before the use of the statistical correlation to compare ... with the standing diversity pattern.
steps.searchable_summary: Compute first-generalized differences for the standing diversity dataset

methods.description: Testing for statistical correlation between the transformed datasets using Spearman's rank-order coefficient, which is suitable for nonnormal variables observed throughout the fossil history.

steps.description: Spearman's rank-order coefficient was used to test for correlation of changes in both the extinction and origination per-capita rates
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Spearman correlation between differenced extinction and origination rates

steps.description: Spearman's rank-order coefficient was used to test for correlation of changes in ... species richness and in turnover rates (i.e., extinction and origination).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Spearman correlation between differenced diversity and differenced turnover rates

methods.description: Testing whether cross-correlations among planktic foraminiferal diversity dynamics time series (diversity pattern and extinction-origination per-capita rates) varied when binned into different global climatic mode intervals.

steps.description: binned into different global climatic mode intervals, comprising a 'mixed house' interval ... (~170 to ~100 Ma), a greenhouse phase (~100 to ~37.5 Ma), and an icehouse phase (~37.5 to 0 Ma)
steps.searchable_summary: Define time intervals corresponding to different climate modes
inputs: Time ranges for climate modes (Mixed house: ~170-100 Ma, Greenhouse: ~100-37.5 Ma, Icehouse: ~37.5-0 Ma)

steps.description: tested whether cross-correlations among planktic foraminiferal diversity dynamics time series (i.e., diversity pattern and extinction-origination per-capita rates) varied when binned into different global climatic mode intervals
steps.searchable_summary: Segment diversity dynamics time series by climate mode intervals
outputs: Segmented time series data for each climate mode

steps.description: tested whether cross-correlations among planktic foraminiferal diversity dynamics time series ... varied when binned into different global climatic mode intervals
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze cross-correlations within each climate mode segment
inputs: Segmented time series data for each climate mode
outputs: Cross-correlation results for each climate mode (e.g., correlation coefficients, significance)

methods.description: Performing a statistical comparison of the complete distribution frequency shapes of both 'turnover-pulse' model data and planktic foraminiferal first and last occurrence data using a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test.

steps.description: statistical comparison of the complete distribution frequency shapes of both 'turnover-pulse' model (Appendix)
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain data representing the 'turnover-pulse' model distribution frequency

steps.description: statistical comparison of the complete distribution frequency shapes of both ... and planktic foraminiferal first and last occurrence data (Appendix).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain distribution frequency data for observed first and last occurrences
inputs: Planktic foraminiferal first and last occurrence data (from Appendix)
outputs: Observed first and last occurrence distribution frequency data

steps.description: a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test for equality of distribution was employed to perform a statistical comparison of the complete distribution frequency shapes of both 'turnover-pulse' model ... and planktic foraminiferal first and last occurrence data
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Kolmogorov-Smirnov test to compare two distribution frequency shapes
inputs: Observed first and last occurrence distribution frequency data
